interaction.prompt: OZ cycle. You won the corner battle and have the puck. Sequence the cycle: tap the players in the order the puck should move.

feedback.right: Wall up to F2, then back to the point — point shoots, you crash. Classic strong-side cycle that bends the box and gets a Grade-A look.
feedback.wrong: Cycles work because they MOVE the kill. Bumping into the slot from the wall is a turnover; reverse to the LD wastes a possession. Wall → mid-wall → point is the read.

tip: Cycle order: corner up the wall, mid-wall to the point, point shoots/passes. Each move pulls a defender, opening the next.
why: Cycles teach cause-and-effect movement. Each pass is meant to drag a defender out of position so the NEXT pass has more room. The point shot is the payoff.
